reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David expressed strong disagreement with the patch and requested a NACK due to concerns that the patch does not address his previous comments about not reimplementing GUP entirely.
reviews.raw_body:
You mean, ignoring my comments about not reiplementing GUP entirely?

NAK

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed frustration that the patch author did not respond to their review, but did not provide any technical feedback or suggestions.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes pointed out that the patch's description of munmap() behavior is incorrect, as it acquires the VMA lock and removes upper-level tables only if the entire range is spanned. Additionally, PTE page tables can be removed via MADV_DONTNEED under RCU, making the GUP fast imitating code unnecessary.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes noted that the patch does not properly handle the transition from per-VMA locks to RCU, specifically pointing out that retract_page_tables() and try_collapse_pte_mapped_thp() still use pte_free_defer(), which should be replaced with RCU-based functions

Reviewer questioned the purpose of the patch, suggesting they may be misunderstanding its intent or that there's a lack of context.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed frustration with the formatting of the patch description, requesting that any AI-generated text be edited for readability.
reviews.raw_body:
Rik, it really would have helped if you'd replied to review :)

On Wed, Jun 24, 2026 at 09:50:52PM -0400, Rik van Riel wrote:

---

Yeah I need to update the documentation on this at
https://docs.kernel.org/mm/process_addrs.html it's more subtle than written
there.

Firstly you're wrong about munmap() - it acquires the VMA lock of the VMAs freed
in the range and will only remove an upper level table if the entire range is
spanned.

And that's the only way higher level tables can be removed.

PTE page tables can be removed via MADV_DONTNEED, but that a. acquires the VMA
lock and b. frees the PTE page table under RCU.

A THP collapse can happen concurrently, but PTEs are freed under RCU so you
don't need to do this GUP fast imitating stuff.

---

retract_page_tables() -> pte_free_defer() -> RCU
try_collapse_pte_mapped_thp() -> pte_free_defer() -> RCU

---

Yes it is?

I mean unless I'm missing something here.

---

This is an unreadable wall of text, if it's AI generated please edit before
sending.

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed disagreement with the patch, suggesting that a discussion is needed in the review itself and implying that the patch may not be necessary.

reviewer requested that the patch author provide a more detailed explanation of how the per-VMA lock is used in __access_remote_vm and how it interacts with other locks, specifically mentioning the need for a clear description of the locking hierarchy
reviews.raw_body:
As I said in reply to 2/3 I don't think you need to do this.

Let's have a discussion _in the review_ about it :)

---

No wall of text please :)

Same comments for the rest, please use paragraphs, try to be succinct, etc.

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the patch series should be sent after the merge window closes and not during it

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed dissatisfaction with the patch description, stating that it does not clearly attribute changes to specific reviewers and implying that the author has not adequately addressed previous comments.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed agreement with the patch but emphasized the importance of responding to reviews inline and having discussions to ensure progress is made correctly, as he has limited review time and prefers triage by discussion rather than dismissal.
reviews.raw_body:
(Note that it's the merge window, really ideally you should be saving sending
non-RFC series until after the merge window closes, none of these series will be
taken yet and you'll have to resend anyway)

On Thu, Jun 25, 2026 at 08:32:43AM +0200, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:

---

This is really not sufficient :) you should break out the changes you
make and who suggested them.

If people give their time to review, you should take the time to make it clear
you've dealt with it.

---

Yeah agreed.

As replied elsewhere, I don't think we need to do that anyway?

But you _really_ need to respond to review inline so we can have those
discussions and ensure that you're moving forward in the correct way.

Just quietly resending with a catch-all comment as per above puts all the load
on us to go and check that you've done what we've asked.

On my side, my review time is very limited now, so triage dictates general
dismissals when the series looks wrong, engaging in discussion will help us all
move forward efficiently.

reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the reliability of system monitoring under mmap_lock contention, specifically during THP collapse. They acknowledge that relying only on the RCU read lock to read the page table could result in seeing old page table contents that no longer point at the current process memory. The author suggests two possible ways for khugepaged to fix this issue and notes that it currently uses the first approach.
reviews.raw_body:
One issue here is that while we can safely read
the old page table under the RCU read lock, in
the middle of a THP collapse there is no guarantee
that the old page table points at the process's 
current memory.

Khugepaged could fix this in one of two ways:
- zap all readers with an IPI, and use that as
  synchronization
- make sure the old page table's PTEs point at
  the individual pages inside the new PMD

Right now khugepaged does the first.

Relying only on the RCU read lock to read the
page table could result in us seeing old page
table contents, that no longer point at the
current process memory.

Unless I'm missing something...

-- 
All Rights Reversed.

reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the feasibility of implementing a mmap_lock-less GUP, expressed willingness to work on it but wants to understand prerequisites and acceptable implementation before proceeding.
reviews.raw_body:
Do we actually have a path to doing that?

I misread that as more of a wish list thing, not
as something we could realistically do today.

How would I go about making that mmap_lock-less
GUP a reality?

What are the prerequisites?

I'm not opposed to working on that, but I
would like to figure out ahead of time
what an acceptable implementation would
roughly look like.

-- 
All Rights Reversed.

2026-06-25.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a fast path for __access_remote_vm that uses per-VMA locks instead of the process-wide mmap_lock, improving system monitoring reliability and speeding up the function under contention. The fast path is only enabled when it's safe to do so, requiring a kernel with per-VMA locks, RCU page table freeing, and specific VMA types excluded. This change aims to address issues where processes get stuck holding the mmap_lock for extended periods, hindering system monitoring tools' ability to assess and log situations.
